body.text: Phoenix Navigator (Node Zero) is an ESP32-S3-based palm device that provides haptic feedback for somatic self-regulation. The hardware prototype is complete; we request mini-grant funding to manufacture fifteen (15) beta units for distribution to Autistic self-advocates and to produce an accessible, plain-language user manual co-authored with the beta cohort.

The Autistic community has been sold expensive closed-source "behavioral intervention" devices (weighted vests, pressure machines, shock collars) that position us as problems to be suppressed. P31 Labs builds the opposite: open, self-owned, self-directed tools that treat Autistic self-regulation as an engineering problem with dignified solutions. Every beta user owns their device and their data. Schematics and firmware are released under AGPLv3.
Plain-language user manual layout + print-ready PDF: $1,500
Compensation for beta cohort (15 × $150 stipend for feedback sessions): $2,250

P31 Labs is founded, incorporated, and operated by an Autistic self-advocate. Every engineering and design decision is made by an Autistic person for Autistic people. The beta cohort will be recruited through ASAN’s chapter network. Cohort members co-author the user manual as paid contributors; their names appear in the project README and on the device documentation.
